message:
fix(useLocalStorage): handle undefined values and non-serializable data

The cleanup flush in useLocalStorage blindly called JSON.stringify on
the latest value and wrote the result to localStorage. If the value was
undefined, JSON.stringify returns undefined (not a string), and
localStorage.setItem with undefined coerces it to the string
"undefined" — which then fails to parse back as valid JSON on next
load, corrupting the stored state.

Now checks for undefined values (removes the key) and verifies
stringify actually returns a string before writing. Non-serializable
values (e.g. containing circular refs) are caught by the existing
try/catch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
